required_patterns: context:;decision:;why not:;fallback:;migration plan:;open questions:;evidence anchors:;replay determinism;customer-managed keys;in-region processing
prompt: Revise an existing architecture memo for a regulated claims orchestration platform. Preserve these exact headings in the final memo: Context, Decision, Why Not, Fallback, Migration Plan, Open Questions, Evidence Anchors. Existing memo flaws: it assumes one shared Kafka cluster and a global event log are fine for every tenant, it treats batch replay during outages as acceptable, and it never quantifies rollback triggers or EU isolation constraints. New repo/runtime evidence you must incorporate: replay determinism breaks when tenants share partitions during regional failover; finance caps infrastructure cost at 3 dollars per active tenant per month; compliance now requires customer-managed keys and in-region processing for EU tenants; synthetic lag spikes above 90 seconds when failover drains backlog. Rewrite the memo with a concrete selected design, one rejected near-miss option, quantified fallback criteria, and explicit evidence anchors.
followup_prompt: Update the same memo after new evidence: EU customers now require strict data residency before quarter end, backlog replay cost doubled during the last failover, and support needs a lower-risk migration window. Keep the same headings, explicitly revise one earlier assumption, and narrow the migration plan accordingly.

required_patterns: summary:;customer impact:;timeline:;root cause:;mitigations:;follow-up owners:;evidence anchors:;retry storm;partial rollback;queue drain
prompt: Rewrite an incident postmortem for a billing API outage. Preserve these exact headings in the final document: Summary, Customer Impact, Timeline, Root Cause, Mitigations, Follow-up Owners, Evidence Anchors. Existing draft flaws: it blames generic traffic growth, omits the failed partial rollback, and never names the concrete owner of the retry-budget mitigation. New evidence you must incorporate: a rate-limit config change triggered a retry storm; the first rollback only reverted one region; queue drain took 47 minutes; EU VIP merchants saw duplicate webhook retries; support macros misled first-line triage for 18 minutes. Rewrite the document with a concrete root cause, one rejected near-miss explanation, tighter timeline sequencing, and explicit follow-up owners.
followup_prompt: Update the same postmortem after new evidence: a config promotion from staging was later confirmed, one earlier timeline event was ten minutes late, and the retry-budget mitigation owner moved from platform to API traffic control. Keep the same headings and explicitly revise the earlier root-cause framing.

prompt: Update an existing failover runbook for a Postgres read replica promotion. Preserve these exact headings in the final runbook: Context, Preconditions, Procedure, Verification, Rollback, Open Risks, Evidence Anchors. Existing runbook flaws: it promotes replicas without checking replay lag, assumes clock skew is harmless, and treats a green TCP probe as enough verification. New evidence you must incorporate: stale promotions occurred when clock skew exceeded 4 seconds; promotion is only safe when replication_state equals streaming and replay lag stays below 15 seconds; backup lock release can lag 2 minutes after role switch; the old rollback step can re-point traffic before read-only mode is restored. Rewrite the runbook with a concrete safer procedure, explicit rollback criteria, and evidence anchors for each critical verification step.
followup_prompt: Update the same runbook after new evidence: replica lag spikes during autovacuum, the maintenance window is now 20 minutes shorter, and on-call wants one lower-risk fallback before traffic cutover. Keep the same headings, revise one earlier assumption, and narrow the procedure accordingly.
